vue.answer:
## Building a Nested Comments Widget in Vue 3

**Mental model:** Comments form a tree (`id`, `text`, `replies[]`) rendered by a self-referencing component. Adding a reply finds the node by id and pushes a child. Recursion handles arbitrary depth.

### Step 1 — Recursive component
A component imports itself to render `replies`.

```vue
<Comment v-for="r in node.replies" :key="r.id" :node="r" @reply="..." />
```

### Step 2 — Reply handler bubbles up
Each comment emits a `reply` event with `(parentId, text)`; the root mutates the tree.

```js
function addReply(nodes, parentId, text) {
  for (const n of nodes) {
    if (n.id === parentId) { n.replies.push({ id: uid(), text, replies: [] }); return true; }
    if (addReply(n.replies, parentId, text)) return true;
  }
  return false;
}
```

### Step 3 — Per-node reply box
A local `open` ref toggles the inline form per comment.

### Step 4 — Commit
Trim, ignore empties, then close the box.

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Deep nesting | Self-referencing component + recursive insert. |
| Empty reply | Trim and skip. |
| Reactivity | Mutating nested arrays is fine in Vue 3 (deep reactive). |

**Interview tip:** Vue 3's deep reactivity means you *can* mutate the nested `replies` array in place (unlike React's immutable copy) — call out that difference; it's a common cross-framework discussion point.

angular.answer:
## Building a Nested Comments Widget in Angular

**Mental model:** Comments form a tree (`id`, `text`, `replies[]`) rendered by a self-referencing component. Adding a reply finds the node by id and pushes a child. A shared service or root handler owns the mutation. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them).

### Step 1 — Recursive component
The component uses its own selector to render `replies`.

```html
<app-comment *ngFor="let r of node.replies" [node]="r" (reply)="reply.emit($event)"></app-comment>
```

### Step 2 — Reply bubbles to the root
Each comment re-emits a `reply` output up the tree; the root inserts.

```typescript
addReply(nodes: Comment[], parentId: string, text: string): boolean {
  for (const n of nodes) {
    if (n.id === parentId) { n.replies.push({ id: uid(), text, replies: [] }); return true; }
    if (this.addReply(n.replies, parentId, text)) { return true; }
  }
  return false;
}
```

### Step 3 — Per-node reply box
A local `open` boolean toggles the inline form.

### Step 4 — Commit
Trim, emit `{ parentId, text }`, close.

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Deep nesting | Self-referencing component + recursive insert. |
| Empty reply | Trim and skip. |
| Re-emit chain | Each level forwards the child's `reply` event upward. |

**Interview tip:** A component that references its own selector is Angular's recursion pattern; bubbling the `reply` output up each level to a single root mutator keeps the insert logic in one place.
